title: Stacked Changes, Maintainer Workflows, and Integration Queues · Graph Lens: Gita Collaboration Policy Defense
summary: A distributed project receives dependent reviews, rerolls, hotfixes, subsystem branches, and release candidates from contributors with different trust and access. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita collaboration policy defense increment in stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define authorship, signoff, review, update, merge, rewrite, rollback, deletion, archival, and exception rules with observable enforcement.
Model base freshness, batch size, speculative testing, queue invalidation, required evidence, and starvation without hiding the exact integrated revision.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Collaboration Policy Defense
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G48 is a theory in a disposable repository. the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies. The learner must predict old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible, run git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2, explain one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent, test this changed condition: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Collaboration Policy Defense contract for the supplied data.

content.text: Define authorship, signoff, review, update, merge, rewrite, rollback, deletion, archival, and exception rules with observable enforcement. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G48 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies. The learner must predict old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible, run git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2, explain one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent, test this changed condition: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published. A defensible solution applies Gita Collaboration Policy Defense, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies
git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2
# expected: old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible
# observation: one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent
# changed-case: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published
# transfer-limit: provider merge queues need separate event, identity, permission, and tested-revision verification
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Collaboration Policy Defense evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-collaboration-policy-defense-78udfw" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Collaboration Policy Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-collaboration-policy-defense-78udfw
# scenario-key: trace-78udfw probe-wfdu87 boundary-udfw78 owner-78udfwx fault-78udfwz
# before-ref: main=a11ce78 candidate=b00b1fw
# hypothesis: the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies
git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2
# expected: old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible
# observation: one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent
# changed-case: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree
# after-ref: main=a11ce78 candidate=c0ffeew
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published
# transfer-limit: provider merge queues need separate event, identity, permission, and tested-revision verification
